hypothesis: If diffusion-based generative models are trained on the preprocessed MP and OQMD datasets, then they will generate crystal structures that are chemically valid and more chemically realistic than controlled perturbations—exhibiting lower RMSD after DFT relaxation, lower energy-above-hull and SUN-instability rates, smaller EMD to the training formation energy and density distributions, and higher validity and precision/recall in latent-space metrics—because the models learn and reproduce underlying chemical and energetic constraints from the data.
